seoTitle: Add a New Person to an Existing Microsoft Teams Chat
metaDescription: Bring a new participant into a Microsoft Teams conversation and control whether they see past chat history when they join.
intro: Expanding a one-on-one Teams chat into a group conversation, or looping in a manager mid-thread, involves a quick decision about how much of the prior conversation the new person should see. This guide covers adding participants and managing chat history privacy at the same time.

steps.heading: Open the conversation
steps.body: Select the individual or group chat you want to add someone to from the left sidebar.

steps.heading: Open the participants panel
steps.body: Click the View and Add Participants icon in the top-right corner of the chat window.

steps.heading: Search for the new person
steps.body: Click Add People, then type their name, contact handle, or external email address.

steps.heading: Choose a chat history option
steps.body: Select whether to include no chat history, a specific number of past days, or the full conversation history for the new member.

steps.heading: Confirm the addition
steps.body: Click Add to finalize the merge and verify the updated member count in the chat.

faq.question: What happens when I add a third person to a one-on-one chat?
faq.answer: Adding a third member automatically converts the conversation into a new, separate group chat.

faq.question: Can I control how much history a new person sees?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose to hide all past messages, share a specific number of days, or release the full chat history.

faq.question: Can I add someone outside my organization to a chat?
faq.answer: Yes, external guest email addresses can be entered the same way as internal directory names.

faq.question: How do I confirm the new person joined successfully?
faq.answer: A confirmation notification banner and an updated member roster count both indicate a successful addition.
